Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MAURY MCFADDEN'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment is granted. Mr. McFadden has shown sufficient grounds under CCP 473 to have the default set aside and doing so comports with California's strong public policy on the merits. Mr. McFadden's signature on the notice and acknowledgement of receipt form constituted good service on him notwithstanding that he may not have understood what he was signing and the form had been altered. Consequently, to avoid unnecessary motion practice, the court advises Mr. McFadden's counsel not to file a motion to quash and instead proceed to respond to the complaint by way of an answer, demurrer or otherwise response that does not contest service of summons and complaint. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
